label: Video generation & simulation synthesis

node.label: Video generation & simulation synthesis
node.definition: Video-generation systems model appearance and motion across time to synthesize, extend, edit, predict, or simulate dynamic visual scenes.
node.why_it_matters: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
examples: workflows combining Video generation & simulation synthesis with Diffusion & score-based models
workflows combining Video generation & simulation synthesis with Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models
workflows combining Video generation & simulation synthesis with Transformers
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Diffusion & score-based models, Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models.

dependencies.rationale: Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Diffusion & score-based models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Normalizing, rectified & flow-matching models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Transformers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Images, video & spatial grids as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Sequences, events & time series as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Generation-time watermarking as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Content credentials, signing & provenance as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
Video generation & simulation synthesis integrates Model servers & inference runtimes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.

sentence_units.text: Video-generation systems model appearance and motion across time to synthesize, extend, edit, predict, or simulate dynamic visual scenes.
This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
